data.body:
/review (4-agent: security/Opus, quality/Sonnet, context/Haiku, freeform/Opus) → PASS, min score 5/5. No must-fix/should-fix. Security: parse_yaml byte-identical, YAML.safe_load posture intact. Quality+Freeform independently ran specs green + verified extend→public-singleton→alias→lexical-scope chain at real boot, eager-load OK, 2 dev reload cycles OK. Context: all 5 ACs met, zero scope creep.

Applied the one convergent finding: added an explicit alias-identity assertion (`described_class::ConfigurationError` equal to `ConfigLoading::ConfigurationError`) to both loader specs — pins the load-bearing invariant that was previously only proven indirectly. Targeted specs now 63 ex 0 failures; RuboCop clean (533 files). Remaining nits deliberately left (require_relative matches certification.rb precedent; global write_yaml include matches env_helpers convention).

PR-body TODO (finding to disclose): the two loaders' ConfigurationError are now one shared object, so a rescue of one would catch the other's — no consumer affected today.
